description: Restructure project as Cargo workspace, add game logic with FIDE end-game detection, UCI move parsing, async engine abstraction, and match orchestration between engines

userStories.title: Convert project to Cargo workspace
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the project to be a Cargo workspace with chesslib as a subcrate, so I can add separate binary crates.
acceptanceCriteria: Root Cargo.toml defines a workspace with members 'chesslib' and 'chess-runner'
chesslib/Cargo.toml contains the lib with all existing code (including build.rs and gen_files)
chess-runner/Cargo.toml defines a binary crate that depends on chesslib
cargo build --workspace compiles without errors
cargo test --workspace passes all existing tests
Benchmarks continue working (cargo bench --bench perft -p chesslib)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Detect checkmate and stalemate
userStories.description: As a lib user, I want to know if a position is checkmate or stalemate to determine end of game.
acceptanceCriteria: Function/method that takes a Board and returns if it is checkmate (no legal moves + in check)
Function/method that returns if it is stalemate (no legal moves + not in check)
Tests with known checkmate positions (Scholar's mate, back rank mate)
Tests with known stalemate positions
Existing perft tests continue passing
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Detect draw by insufficient material
userStories.description: As a lib user, I want to detect draw by insufficient material (K vs K, K+B vs K, K+N vs K, K+B vs K+B same color).
acceptanceCriteria: Function that analyzes remaining material and returns if it is insufficient
Covers cases: K vs K, K+N vs K, K+B vs K, K+B vs K+B (bishops on same color)
Tests for each insufficient material case
Tests for positions with sufficient material (does not return draw)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement Zobrist hashing for Board
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Zobrist hashing on Board so that position repetition can be detected efficiently.
acceptanceCriteria: Zobrist keys generated as constants (in build.rs or const): one key per piece/square combination, keys for castling rights, en passant files, and side to move
Zobrist hashing includes a key for Side to Move to ensure identical positions with different turns have different hashes
Board stores its current Zobrist hash
Hash is updated incrementally in make_move() via XOR
Board exposes method to get current hash (e.g. board.zobrist_hash() -> u64)
Tests verifying that the same position reached by different move orders produces the same hash
Tests verifying different positions produce different hashes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Halfmove clock on Board
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Board to track the halfmove clock so 50/75-move rules can be detected.
acceptanceCriteria: Board stores halfmove_clock field, initialized from FEN (6th token)
halfmove_clock is reset to 0 on pawn moves and captures in make_move()
halfmove_clock is incremented by 1 on all other moves in make_move()
Board exposes method to get halfmove clock
Board::default() starts with halfmove_clock = 0
Tests for halfmove clock incrementing and resetting correctly
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Game struct with move history and result tracking
userStories.description: As a lib user, I want a Game struct representing a complete game with history, so I can track game progress.
acceptanceCriteria: Game contains: current Board, list of moves played (Vec<ChessMove>), game result, and position hash history (Vec<u64> from Zobrist)
Enum GameResult with variants: Ongoing, WhiteWins, BlackWins, Draw(DrawReason)
DrawReason with variants: Stalemate, InsufficientMaterial, ThreefoldRepetition, FivefoldRepetition, FiftyMoveRule, SeventyFiveMoveRule
Game::new() from a FEN string or default starting position

Method make_move() that validates legality, applies the move, updates history, hash history, and detects end of game (checkmate, stalemate, insufficient material, threefold/fivefold repetition, 50/75-move rule)
Fivefold repetition and 75-move rule are applied automatically as draws
Threefold repetition and 50-move rule are exposed as queryable but also applied automatically
Method to get current Board
Method to get move history
Tests for a complete game (opening to mate)
Tests for draw detection via repetition (make moves that repeat position 3 and 5 times)
Tests for 50-move and 75-move rule detection
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-002 (checkmate/stalemate), US-003 (insufficient material), US-004 (Zobrist), US-005 (halfmove clock)

userStories.title: Clock struct with time control and increment
userStories.description: As a lib user, I want Game to support time control with increment for UCI compatibility.
acceptanceCriteria: Struct Clock with remaining time per player (in milliseconds) and increment
Clock is updated each move: subtracts elapsed time (using std::time::Instant), adds increment
Support for configurations: total time + increment, moves to go (for classical time controls)
Detection of flag (time expired) as end of game condition
Game can be created with or without a clock (clock is optional)
Tests for time decrement and flag detection
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: UCI move parsing and serialization
userStories.description: As a lib user, I want to convert between ChessMove and UCI string (e.g. e2e4, e7e8q) to communicate with engines.
acceptanceCriteria: ChessMove::from_uci(s: &str, board: &Board) -> Result<ChessMove> — needs board to resolve promotion ambiguity and validate legality
ChessMove::to_uci() -> String — serializes to UCI format
Format: {source}{dest} (4 chars) or {source}{dest}{promotion} (5 chars, promotion lowercase: q/r/b/n)
Tests for normal moves, captures, castling (e1g1, e1c1), en passant, and promotions
Tests for invalid UCI strings returning errors
Roundtrip test: ChessMove -> UCI string -> ChessMove preserves the original move
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Async Engine trait for engine abstraction
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an async Engine trait that abstracts engine communication, to plug in-process or UCI engines.
acceptanceCriteria: Trait Engine with async methods: name() -> String, new_game(), set_position(game: &Game), go(time_control: &TimeControl) -> ChessMove, quit()
TimeControl struct that encapsulates wtime, btime, winc, binc, movestogo
Trait uses async fn with tokio as the runtime
Add tokio as dependency to chesslib (with appropriate features)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: UciEngine subprocess communication
userStories.description: As a user, I want to instantiate a UCI engine from an executable path to use engines like Stockfish.
acceptanceCriteria: Struct UciEngine that implements trait Engine
Spawns engine process using tokio::process::Command and communicates via stdin/stdout
Implements UCI handshake: sends 'uci', waits for 'uciok'
Implements 'isready'/'readyok' for synchronization
Sends 'ucinewgame' in new_game() to clear state between games
Sends 'position startpos moves e2e4 e7e5 ...' with full move history
Sends 'go wtime X btime Y winc Z binc W' with clock information
stdout reader consumes/discards all intermediate lines (like 'info') until finding and extracting the 'bestmove' line
Parses 'bestmove e2e4' from response
Sends 'quit' on drop/quit
Configurable timeout for engine responses
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Tests may require a mock engine or simple UCI-compliant engine available in the environment

userStories.title: Match orchestrator for engine vs engine games
userStories.description: As a user, I want to run a complete game between two engines to compare engines or test the lib.
acceptanceCriteria: Struct Match that takes two Box<dyn Engine>, a Game, and clock configuration
Game loop: asks move from engine of side to move, applies to Game, checks end of game, passes to next engine
Updates clock each move
Terminates on: checkmate, stalemate, draw (any type), flag (time expired), or engine error
Threefold repetition and 50-move rule are applied automatically as draws by the Match
Returns GameResult at the end
Callback or trait to observe the game (e.g. print each move)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: chess-runner CLI binary
userStories.description: As a user, I want a CLI that runs a match between two UCI engines to test the full integration.
acceptanceCriteria: Accepts arguments: paths of two engines, time control (time + increment)
Instantiates two UciEngine, creates a Match, runs the game
Prints each move in UCI notation and the board (debug format) during the game
Prints final result (winner, draw reason, or error)
Supports match of N games (--games N), alternating colors between engines each game
Accepts optional --start-fen argument to start all games from a specific position
At the end of the match, prints total score (wins/losses/draws per engine)
Example: cargo run -p chess-runner -- --engine1 /path/to/stockfish --engine2 /path/to/engine2 --time 60000 --inc 1000 --games 10
Typecheck passes
